name: env-continuity-dedup-v2
description: Distributional baseline for environmental continuity block size (continuity_block_chars) across multiple biome archetypes on Turn 1. Each run starts a fresh session with a founding premise placing the player in a distinct environmental archetype (town street, alley, coastal boardwalk, desert road, cobblestone village). Measures whether the env-fact dedup pass in ContinuityBrain keeps the continuity block at a reasonable size even on the first turn, where env facts from the founding premise are injected. A single-turn probe by design — multi-turn bloat regression is covered by the env_continuity_bloat_walk harness scenario. post_extract is intentionally omitted: computeMetrics reads continuity_block_chars from the primary /narrate response (debug.narration_debug.continuity_block_chars) via the response fallback path.
prompt_cycle: I am standing on Main Street in a quiet town, in front of a row of weathered buildings with cracked pavement and a red-and-white awning. A rusted mailbox stands crooked nearby.
I am walking along a cobblestone lane in an old village. Stone cottages with thatched roofs line both sides. A wooden sign creaks above a pub door.
I stand at the edge of a dusty desert road. Adobe buildings with sun-bleached walls stretch ahead. A worn cactus fence borders the path.
I am on the wooden boardwalk of a coastal town. Salt-worn shop fronts with faded signs face the harbor. A bell buoy clangs in the distance.
I am in a narrow alley between tall brick buildings. Fire escapes zigzag up the walls. A single streetlamp flickers overhead.
